2604.20443.title: DialToM: A Theory of Mind Benchmark for Forecasting State-Driven Dialogue Trajectories
2604.20443.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have been shown to possess Theory of Mind (ToM) abilities. However, it remains unclear whether this stems from robust reasoning or spurious correlations. We introduce DialToM, a human-verified benchmark built from natural human dialogue using a multiple-choice framework. We evaluate not only mental state prediction (Literal ToM) but also the functional utility of these states (Functional ToM) through Prospective Diagnostic Forecasting -- probing whether models can identify state-consistent dialogue trajectories solely from mental-state profiles. Our results reveal a significant reasoning asymmetry: while LLMs excel at identifying mental states, most (except for Gemini 3 Pro) fail to leverage this understanding to forecast social trajectories. Additionally, we find only weak semantic similarities between human and LLM-generated inferences. To facilitate reproducibility, the DialToM dataset and evaluation code are publicly available at https://github.com/Stealth-py/DialToM.
2604.20443.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）已被證明具備心智理論（ToM）能力。<lb/>然而，這是否源於強大的推理能力或虛假的相關性仍不清楚。<lb/>我們介紹了DialToM，一個基於自然人類對話構建的人類驗證基準，採用多選框架。<lb/>我們不僅評估心理狀態預測（字面ToM），還通過前瞻性診斷預測評估這些狀態的功能實用性（功能ToM）——探查模型是否能僅根據心理狀態檔案識別狀態一致的對話軌跡。<lb/>我們的結果顯示出顯著的推理不對稱：儘管LLMs在識別心理狀態方面表現出色，但大多數（除了Gemini 3 Pro）未能利用這一理解來預測社交軌跡。<lb/>此外，我們發現人類和LLM生成的推論之間只有微弱的語義相似性。<lb/>為了促進可重現性，DialToM數據集和評估代碼已公開提供於 https://github.com/Stealth-py/DialToM。
2604.20443.author: Neemesh Yadav et.al.
2604.20443.authors: Neemesh Yadav, Palakorn Achananuparp, Jing Jiang, Ee-Peng Lim
